div.head: The Ambassador in China (Johnson) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
204. The Consul General at MukdenJoseph W. Ballantine. reported in despatch 247, April 14,Not printed. that he had advised Americans residing at Sinpin to
evacuate. Prior to issuing the warning, Ballantine had been informed by the commander of the gendarmes at Mukden that banditry was rife in the Sinpin area and that there were not enough troops available to afford adequate protection. A part of the danger apparently arose from the attitude of the authorities toward the missionaries at Sinpin, some evidence of which is contained in Mukden’s despatch 234, March 12.Not printed. It is understood that of the five or six Americans who were at Sinpin, only one or two remain and that those remaining expect to evacuate within 1 or 2 days.
